steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage claims most home lands are much smaller than the nearly-100-square-mile maximum, then gives Kalamaula (7.95 sq mi) as the first example. The blank must give Makuu's total area from the table — and that value must also be small to support the claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘3.44 square miles.’: The table lists Makuu at 3.44 square miles — well under 100, consistent with the "much smaller" claim. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘37.26 square miles.’: This is the area of Kahikaiui, not Makuu. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘15.99 square miles.’: This is the area of Kawailoa, not Makuu. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘23.57 square miles.’: This is the area of Kaena, not Makuu. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
